title: Walls and Gates
prompt: You are given an m x n grid `rooms` initialized with -1 for walls, 0 for gates, and 2147483647 (INT_MAX) for empty rooms. Fill each empty room with the distance to its nearest gate (4-directional). If unreachable, leave INT_MAX. Modify in-place and return the grid.
hints: Multi-source BFS starting from every gate simultaneously.
Distance increments by 1 each BFS layer; only update INT_MAX cells.

alternatives.note: Too slow on worst-case sparse grids.

pitfalls: DFS without distance tracking can revisit cells with worse paths.
Updating walls or gates by mistake.
followups: With multiple cell types and obstacles.
